title: hieracium atratum subsp. extumidum
intro:
Hieracium atratum subsp. extumidum, commonly known as Hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. The therapeutic actions of Hieracium atratum subsp. extumidum include its ability to exhibit antioxidant, antispasmodic, and anti-edematous effects, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

However, I must note that I couldn't find any extensive information on this specific subspecies of Hieracium atratum.
benefits: Hieracium atratum subsp. extumidum has many benefits, such as its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and ease pain in individuals suffering from arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, it has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and improve overall health by reducing the risk of chronic diseases like cancer, diabetes, and heart disease. Its antimicrobial properties also make it effective in treating wounds and infections, while its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may even help to improve cognitive function and reduce the risk of age-related neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. By incorporating Hieracium atratum subsp. extumidum into one's daily routine, individuals may experience improved overall health, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, ultimately leading to a better quality of life.
parts: Hieracium atratum subsp. extumidum has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, flowers, and roots, which have been traditionally used in herbal medicine. The leaves of Hieracium atratum subsp. extumidum are rich in flavonoids, particularly quercetin and luteolin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers contain sesquiterpene lactones, including heliangolide and pseudogamma-lactone, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The roots of Hieracium atratum subsp. extumidum contain caffeic acid and its derivatives, which may help to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms and exhibit antioxidant effects.
side_effects: Hieracium atratum subsp. extumidum can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can also lead to headaches, dizziness, and fatigue due to its potential interference with blood clotting and liver function. Additionally, improper use of this plant may cause skin irritation, including redness and itching, especially for individuals with sensitive skin. In severe cases, excessive consumption of Hieracium atratum subsp. extumidum can lead to allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis.
